executive_summary.description: A high-level overview of the comparative analysis of Python code-graph extraction tools, summarizing the key findings, the state of the market in 2026, and the final recommendation.

primary_extractor.description: The primary tool recommended for v1 extraction.

complementary_indexer.description: A secondary tool recommended to complement the primary extractor.

fast_fallback.description: A fast, lightweight tool recommended for quick previews or as a fallback.

deferred_to_v2.description: Capabilities or integrations that are recommended to be deferred to a future v2 release.

dynamic_dispatch_heavy_codebases_miss_ra.description: The estimated percentage of runtime call edges that will be missed in dynamic-dispatch-heavy codebases.

typical_codebases_miss_rate.description: The estimated percentage of runtime call edges that will be missed in typical codebases with moderate dynamic features.

dynamic_dispatch_heavy_codebases_recover.description: The target percentage of edges to be recovered in dynamic-dispatch-heavy projects.

well_typed_codebases_recovery_rate.description: The target percentage of edges to be recovered in well-typed projects.

python_v1_deferral_tradeoff.description: An analysis of the strategic trade-off of deferring Python support entirely to a v2 release and shipping a TypeScript-only v1, considering user value, engineering risk, and roadmap implications.

tool_name.description: The name of the code-graph extraction tool.

maintenance_status_2024_2026.description: The maintenance status and development activity between 2024 and 2026.

accuracy_on_dynamic_features.description: Analysis of the tool's accuracy on dynamic dispatch, decorators, metaclasses, and async code.

type_hint_support.description: Whether and how the tool uses type hints to improve accuracy.

output_formats.description: The graph or data formats the tool can emit (e.g., DOT, JSON, SCIP).

installation_method.description: How the tool is installed (e.g., pip, npm, cargo).

licensing.description: The software license of the tool (e.g., MIT, GPL-2.0, Apache-2.0).

suitability_score.description: A score or qualitative assessment of the tool's suitability for the project.

tool_comparative_analysis.description: A deep, comparative analysis of the primary Python code-graph extraction tools. Each item in the array will represent a single tool and provide detailed information on its maintenance status, last meaningful release timeline (2024-2026), accuracy on dynamic dispatch, decorators, metaclasses, async, and type-hint-driven inference, supported output formats (DOT, JSON, SCIP), installation complexity, licensing, and recent commit activity. The tools covered are pyan3, scip-python, pycg, and jarvis.

extractable_from_ast.description: What can be reliably extracted from the Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) alone, without semantic analysis.

requires_semantic_info.description: What features require semantic or type information to be accurately extracted.

expected_miss_rate.description: The expected miss-rate on call edges for dynamic-dispatch-heavy codebases.

estimated_effort.description: The estimated development effort in person-months to build a production-grade extractor.

maintenance_burden.description: The expected long-term maintenance burden, including tracking grammar changes.

licensing.description: Licensing constraints of using tree-sitter and its Python grammar.

recommendation_for_v1.description: A go/no-go recommendation for building a custom extractor for v1.

tool_name.description: The name of the type checker (e.g., pytype, Pyre, mypy).

call_graph_extraction_mechanism.description: The mechanism to extract call edges, such as APIs, plugins, or command-line flags.

accuracy_on_dynamic_dispatch.description: The tool's accuracy in resolving dynamic dispatch, with and without type hints.

output_formats.description: The output formats available for call graph data.

maintenance_status_2024_2026.description: The development activity and long-term support signals for the tool from 2024-2026.

licensing.description: The software license and any redistribution implications.

server_name.description: The name of the Language Server Protocol (LSP) server (e.g., pyright, pylsp, Jedi).

exposed_data.description: The data exposed by the server that can be used to infer call edges (e.g., definitions, references).

dynamic_dispatch_coverage.description: The server's coverage of dynamic dispatch and its reliance on type hints.

output_possibilities.description: Potential output formats, such as raw LSP JSON or SCIP export.

maintenance_status_2024_2026.description: The commit activity and active development status from 2024-2026.

licensing.description: The software license of the LSP server.

integration_complexity.description: The complexity of integrating the LSP server with a TypeScript plugin.

repository_details.description: Details about the tool's repository and how it is distributed (e.g., PyPI package name).

output_format.description: The format of the call graph data produced by the tool.

maintenance_status.description: The maintenance status and release history of the tool.

nuanced_selection_rationale.description: The reasons why the company Nuanced reportedly chose this tool.

nuanced_validation_2026.description: Whether Nuanced's subsequent actions or statements in 2026 validated their choice of this tool.

company_name.description: The name of the emerging company or tool, such as Nuanced.

founded_year.description: The year the company was founded.

description.description: A brief description of the company and its product offerings.

key_features.description: A summary of the key features of the tool or platform.

open_source_component.description: Information about any open-source libraries or components released by the company.

integration_points.description: How the tool integrates with other products, such as AI coding agents.

dynamic_dispatch_coverage_comparison.description: A focused, cross-cutting comparison summarizing the accuracy and effectiveness of each evaluated tool and strategy in handling dynamic dispatch, which is noted as a classic failure mode for static analysis.

output_format_integration_comparison.description: A comparative summary of the graph formats emitted by each tool (e.g., DOT, JSON, SCIP proto, plain edges) and an analysis of their suitability and ease of integration with a TypeScript-based plugin.

licensing_implications_summary.description: A summary of the licensing models (e.g., GPL, MIT, Apache 2.0) for each tool and the potential implications for redistribution within a commercial, proprietary product.

maintenance_and_activity_comparison_2024.description: A comparative overview of the maintenance status and development velocity for each tool, specifically focusing on commit activity, releases, and community support between 2024 and 2026.
